failed_cases.description: Requirement parses direct URLs and marker text as distinct fields.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('Demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"')
print(r.name)
print(r.url)
print(str(r.specifier))
print(type(r.marker).__name__)
print(str(r))
failed_cases.expected_output:
demo
https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl

Marker
demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl; python_version >= "3.10"
failed_cases.actual:
demo
https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl

Marker
demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"
failed_cases.expected:
demo
https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl

Marker
demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl; python_version >= "3.10"

failed_cases.description: Requirement equality and hashing use normalized names and sorted extras.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
a = Requirement('Pkg_Name[B,A]>=1')
b = Requirement('pkg-name[a,b] >= 1.0.0')
print(a == b)
print(len({a, b}))
print(str(b))

failed_cases.description: Canonical requirement serialization can be parsed again without changing extras, specifier, or marker semantics.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
a = Requirement('Pkg_B[Z,A] >=1.0 ; python_version >= "3.8"')
b = Requirement(str(a))
print(str(a))
print(a == b)
print(is_requirement_satisfied(b, '1.0.0', {'python_version': '3.11'}))

all_cases.description: Public names import from the single module and exception classes inherit from ValueError.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version, InvalidVersion, SpecifierSet, InvalidSpecifier, Requirement, InvalidRequirement, Marker, InvalidMarker, UndefinedEnvironmentName, default_environment, is_requirement_satisfied
print(Version.__name__, SpecifierSet.__name__, Requirement.__name__, Marker.__name__)
print(issubclass(InvalidVersion, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidSpecifier, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidRequirement, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidMarker, ValueError), issubclass(UndefinedEnvironmentName, ValueError))
all_cases.expected_output:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker
True True True True True
all_cases.actual:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker
True True True True True
all_cases.expected:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker
True True True True True

all_cases.description: Version canonicalizes release zeros, epoch, pre-release aliases, post-release aliases, and local labels.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
vals = ['1.0.0', '2!01.02.0', '1.0ALPHA2', '1.0-r3', '1.0+LOCAL_build-5']
print([str(Version(v)) for v in vals])
print(Version('1.0') == Version('1.0.0'))

all_cases.description: Version ordering handles dev, pre, final, and post releases.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
ordered = [Version(v) for v in ['1.0.dev1', '1.0a1', '1.0b1', '1.0rc1', '1.0', '1.0.post1']]
print(all(a < b for a, b in zip(ordered, ordered[1:])))
print([str(v) for v in sorted(reversed(ordered))])

all_cases.description: Version ordering includes epochs and local labels after public-version equality.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
print(Version('1!0.9') > Version('2.0'))
print(Version('1.0+abc') < Version('1.0+1'))
print(Version('1.0+1') < Version('1.0+2'))

all_cases.description: Invalid version text raises InvalidVersion.
SpecifierSet applies inclusive and exclusive range clauses.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.2, <2.0')
print(str(s))
print([s.contains(v) for v in ['1.1', '1.2', '1.9', '2.0']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports equality, inequality, and wildcard release prefixes.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
print([SpecifierSet('==1.2.*').contains(v) for v in ['1.2', '1.2.5', '1.3']])
print([SpecifierSet('!=1.2.*').contains(v) for v in ['1.1', '1.2.9', '1.3']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports compatible release bounds.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s1 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4')
s2 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4.5')
print([s1.contains(v) for v in ['1.4', '1.9', '2.0']])
print([s2.contains(v) for v in ['1.4.5', '1.4.9', '1.5']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet excludes pre-releases by default and includes them when requested.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.0')
print(s.contains('1.1a1'))
print(s.contains('1.1a1', prereleases=True))
print(s.contains('1.1.dev1', prereleases=False))

all_cases.description: Invalid specifier text raises InvalidSpecifier.
Requirement parses normalized names, sorted normalized extras, and canonical specifiers.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('My_Pkg[PDF,security-extra] >=1.0, <2')
print(r.name)
print(sorted(r.extras))
print(str(r.specifier))
print(str(r))

all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('Demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"')
print(r.name)
print(r.url)
print(str(r.specifier))
print(type(r.marker).__name__)
print(str(r))
all_cases.expected_output:
demo
https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl

Marker
demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl; python_version >= "3.10"
all_cases.actual:
demo
https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl

Marker
demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"
all_cases.expected:

all_cases.description: Requirement equality and hashing use normalized names and sorted extras.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
a = Requirement('Pkg_Name[B,A]>=1')
b = Requirement('pkg-name[a,b] >= 1.0.0')
print(a == b)
print(len({a, b}))
print(str(b))

all_cases.description: Invalid requirement strings raise InvalidRequirement.
Marker evaluates equality and version comparisons against a caller environment.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
env = {'python_version': '3.11', 'os_name': 'posix'}
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.8"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('os_name == "nt"').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Marker honors boolean precedence and parentheses.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
env = {'python_version': '3.9', 'sys_platform': 'linux', 'os_name': 'posix'}
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.8" and sys_platform == "linux"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('python_version < "3.8" and (sys_platform == "linux" or os_name == "posix")').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Marker evaluates membership operators and requested extras.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
print(Marker('sys_platform in "linux darwin"').evaluate({'sys_platform': 'linux'}))
print(Marker('extra == "security-extra"').evaluate({}, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))
print(Marker('extra == "docs"').evaluate({}, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))

all_cases.description: Marker syntax errors and missing environment variables use the documented exceptions.
A requirement's parsed specifier must evaluate installed versions using the shared Version semantics.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
r = Requirement('demo>=1.0,<2')
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.0.0'))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '2.0'))

all_cases.description: Version-valued marker comparisons must use the same ordering as Version.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker, Version
env = {'python_version': '3.10', 'python_full_version': '3.10.0rc1'}
print(Version('3.10.0rc1') < Version('3.10'))
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.9"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('python_full_version < "3.10"').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Requested extras and requirement marker extras normalize to the same extra namespace.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
r = Requirement('crypto-lib>=2; extra == "security-extra"')
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '2.1', requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.9', requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.9', requested_extras={'docs'}))

all_cases.description: A direct-URL requirement keeps URL parsing separate while its marker still controls applicability.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
r = Requirement('tool @ https://example.test/tool.whl ; sys_platform == "linux"')
print(r.url)
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '0', {'sys_platform': 'linux'}))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '0', {'sys_platform': 'win32'}))

all_cases.description: Compatible-release specifiers parsed from requirements must compute upper bounds from normalized release segments.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
r = Requirement('lib~=1.4.5')
print(str(r.specifier))
print([is_requirement_satisfied(r, v) for v in ['1.4.5', '1.4.9', '1.5']])

all_cases.description: A parsed requirement object can be reused across environments and versions without retaining prior evaluation state.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
r = Requirement('pkg>=1; python_version >= "3.10"')
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.2', {'python_version': '3.11'}))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.2', {'python_version': '3.9'}))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '0.9', {'python_version': '3.11'}))

all_cases.description: Prerelease handling is consistent whether a specifier is used directly or through requirement satisfaction.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet, Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
s = SpecifierSet('>=1')
r = Requirement('pkg>=1')
print(s.contains('1.1a1'))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.1a1'))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.1a1', prereleases=True))

all_cases.description: Failed parsing and missing marker environment errors do not corrupt later valid requirement satisfaction.
Boolean marker precedence controls whether a version specifier is applied to a requirement.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
env = {'python_version': '3.9', 'sys_platform': 'linux', 'os_name': 'nt'}
r1 = Requirement('pkg>=2; python_version >= "3.10" and sys_platform == "linux" or os_name == "nt"')
r2 = Requirement('pkg>=2; python_version >= "3.10" and (sys_platform == "linux" or os_name == "nt")')
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r1, '1.0', env))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r2, '1.0', env))

all_cases.description: Canonical requirement serialization can be parsed again without changing extras, specifier, or marker semantics.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
a = Requirement('Pkg_B[Z,A] >=1.0 ; python_version >= "3.8"')
b = Requirement(str(a))
print(str(a))
print(a == b)
print(is_requirement_satisfied(b, '1.0.0', {'python_version': '3.11'}))

all_cases.description: Marker evaluation for requested extras must not mutate the caller's environment dictionary.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
env = {'python_version': '3.11'}
r = Requirement('pkg>=1; extra == "docs" or python_version >= "3.10"')
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.2', env, requested_extras={'Docs'}))
print(sorted(env.items()))

all_cases.description: Wildcard specifiers and platform markers parsed from one requirement string must compose without losing either constraint.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied
r = Requirement('plugin==1.2.*; sys_platform in "linux darwin"')
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.2.9', {'sys_platform': 'linux'}))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.3', {'sys_platform': 'linux'}))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, '1.3', {'sys_platform': 'win32'}))
